questions.question_text: How many members serve on the Iowa Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: IREC consists of 5 members appointed by the Governor: 4 licensed real estate professionals and 1 public member (Iowa Code 543B.8, as amended in 2024). All appointments are subject to confirmation by a two-thirds vote of the Iowa Senate.

questions.question_text: How are IREC members confirmed after the Governor nominates them?
questions.correct_answer: Two-thirds vote of the Iowa Senate
distractors: Simple majority of the Iowa House
questions.explanation: All IREC members are appointed by the Governor and must be confirmed by a two-thirds vote of the Iowa Senate. This confirmation process provides a check on the appointment power.

questions.question_text: A broker has served two full terms on IREC and is nominated for a third. Can she accept the appointment?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, 3 full terms is the maximum
questions.explanation: IREC members may serve a maximum of 3 full terms, totaling 9 years (3-year terms). Since the broker has served only 2 full terms, she is eligible for one more appointment.

questions.question_text: IREC includes both licensed professionals and public members. How many of the 5 seats are reserved for public members?
questions.explanation: Of IREC's 5 members, 4 must be licensed real estate professionals and 1 is a public member who represents the general public. At least one of the licensed members must be a salesperson. This mix ensures both industry expertise and consumer perspective.

questions.question_text: IREC's authority to regulate real estate licensing derives from which Iowa statute?
questions.explanation: Iowa Code Chapter 543B authorizes the Iowa Real Estate Commission and governs real estate licensing. The commission's rules, historically numbered 193E, were transferred to the Department of Inspections, Appeals, and Licensing rules (Iowa Admin. Code agency 481) in 2026. Chapter 558A covers seller disclosures, not licensing.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain an Iowa real estate salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Iowa requires salesperson applicants to be at least 18 years of age. This is consistent with the legal age to enter into contracts in Iowa.

questions.question_text: A candidate completes Iowa's pre-license course and passes the PSI exam. Within what timeframe must she apply for her license?
questions.explanation: Iowa applicants must submit their license application within 6 months of passing the PSI exam. After 6 months, the exam results expire and the candidate must retake the examination.

questions.question_text: A salesperson applicant asks if she can apply for an inactive license without a sponsoring broker. What is the correct answer?
questions.correct_answer: No, a sponsoring broker is required
distractors: Yes, inactive status needs no broker
Yes, for the first 6 months only
questions.explanation: Iowa does not allow salesperson applicants to apply for an inactive license. A sponsoring broker must be identified on the application. The applicant cannot practice or hold a license without broker sponsorship.

questions.question_text: Which costs must an Iowa salesperson applicant pay to get licensed?
questions.explanation: Iowa salesperson applicants pay a $125 license fee with the application plus a $51 fingerprint/background check fee, paid when the background check is initiated (the completed check must be within 210 days of the application). The $170 fee applies to broker applications.

questions.question_text: An applicant had her license revoked in Nebraska 18 months ago. Can she apply for an Iowa salesperson license?
questions.correct_answer: No, revocation bars her for 2 years
distractors: Yes, other states don't affect Iowa
Yes, if she retakes pre-license education
No, revocation is a permanent bar
questions.explanation: Iowa denies applications from anyone whose license was revoked in any state within the preceding 2 years. Since this applicant's revocation was only 18 months ago, she must wait an additional 6 months before applying.

questions.question_text: How many months of active salesperson experience does Iowa require before applying for a broker license?
questions.explanation: Iowa requires at least 24 months of active experience as a licensed salesperson preceding the broker application (Iowa Code 543B.15(7)). Equivalent experience — such as active licensure in another state, or substantially equal experience as a former broker or real estate manager — can also satisfy the requirement.

questions.question_text: A salesperson wants to upgrade to a broker license. How many additional education hours must she complete beyond salesperson pre-license?
questions.explanation: Iowa requires 60 additional hours of broker-specific education completed within 24 months of taking the broker exam. This is in addition to the 60-hour salesperson pre-license course.

questions.question_text: A broker candidate passes the national portion of the exam but fails the state portion. What happens?
questions.correct_answer: She must retake the entire exam
distractors: She retakes only the state portion
The national score is banked for 12 months
questions.explanation: Iowa's broker exam is scored as a whole: a candidate needs a combined score of 90 (out of 120 points) including at least 30 of 40 on the state portion. A candidate who does not pass must re-register with PSI, pay the exam fee again, and retake the entire examination — Iowa does not bank a passed portion.

questions.question_text: A licensed agent from Illinois wants an Iowa license. Under Iowa's nonresident licensing law, what must she provide?
questions.explanation: Nonresident applicants must file a certification of licensure from their home state showing they are currently licensed with no pending charges (Iowa Code 543B.21). The exam may be waived only for nonresident brokers from states with reciprocity agreements. All nonresidents must also complete the Nonresident Consent section of the application.

questions.question_text: What is the application fee for an Iowa broker license?
questions.explanation: The Iowa broker license application fee is $170, which is $45 more than the $125 salesperson application fee. This is separate from the $51 fingerprint/background check fee also required.
